# Embedded Systems: The Role of Embedded Systems in Modern IoT Applications

![The Role of Embedded Systems in Modern IoT Applications](data:image/svg+xml;nitro-empty-id=Nzc0Ojk3NA==-1;base64,PHN2ZyB2aWV3Qm94PSIwIDAgNzY4IDQzMiIgd2lkdGg9Ijc2OCIgaGVpZ2h0PSI0MzIiIHhtbG5zPSJodHRwOi8vd3d3LnczLm9yZy8yMDAwL3N2ZyI+PC9zdmc+)

The Internet of Things (IoT) revolutionizes industries by connecting physical devices to the digital world. At the heart of this revolution lies the embedded system, a specialized computer system designed to perform specific tasks within a larger system. These intelligent devices, equipped with sensors and connectivity, enable seamless communication and data exchange, unlocking various possibilities across various sectors.

In this blog, we’ll delve into the critical role of [embedded systems](https://www.arigs.com/services/embedded-engineering-services/) in powering the IoT.

## Understanding Embedded Systems

Embedded systems are the brains behind countless devices, from smartphones to industrial machinery. They are specialized computer systems designed to perform specific tasks within larger systems.

At the heart of an embedded system lies a microprocessor, the device’s “brain,” responsible for processing information. Memory, input/output interfaces, and a real-time operating system (RTOS) work in tandem to ensure the system functions efficiently and reliably.

## Key Features of Embedded Systems in IoT

Embedded systems are the backbone of IoT devices, enabling them to collect, process, and transmit data efficiently. To fulfil these roles, they possess several crucial features:

## Challenges and Considerations in Embedded Systems

While embedded systems offer immense potential, they also present several challenges:

## Future Trends: Shaping the Future of Embedded Systems

The future of embedded systems is poised for significant advancements, driven by emerging technologies and evolving industry needs. Here are some key trends that will shape the landscape:

By integrating AI and ML capabilities directly into embedded systems, we can unlock a new era of intelligent edge devices. These systems will be capable of making real-time decisions, adapting to changing conditions, and optimizing performance. This will lead to more autonomous and responsive devices, revolutionizing industries like healthcare, automotive, and manufacturing.

Edge computing is emerging as a critical paradigm, enabling data processing and analysis closer to the source. This reduces latency, enhances privacy, and improves system responsiveness. Embedded systems will play a pivotal role in edge computing, acting as intelligent gateways to collect, process, and transmit data locally.

The advent of 5G and subsequent generations of wireless technology will usher in a new era of high-speed, low-latency connectivity. [Embedded systems](https://www.arigs.com/services/embedded-engineering-services/) will leverage these advancements to enable real-time data exchange, remote control, and advanced IoT applications. This will drive innovation in areas such as autonomous vehicles, smart cities, and industrial automation.

As these trends converge, embedded systems will become increasingly sophisticated and versatile. By embracing innovation and addressing emerging challenges, we can unlock the full potential of embedded systems and shape a future where technology seamlessly integrates with our lives.

## Conclusion:

**In Conclusion: The Indispensable Role of Embedded Systems in Modern IoT**

Embedded systems are the unsung heroes of the IoT revolution. These specialized computer systems, designed to perform specific tasks within larger devices, are the brains behind countless IoT applications. From smart homes and wearables to industrial automation and autonomous vehicles, embedded systems are the connective tissue that brings these technologies to life.

By seamlessly integrating hardware and software components, embedded systems enable devices to collect, process, and transmit data efficiently. This data-driven approach empowers organizations to make informed decisions, optimize operations, and enhance user experiences. Moreover, embedded systems play a crucial role in ensuring the security and reliability of IoT devices, safeguarding sensitive information and preventing unauthorized access.

As the IoT landscape continues to evolve, the demand for sophisticated embedded systems will only increase. By staying abreast of emerging technologies and industry trends, developers and engineers can create innovative solutions that drive digital transformation and shape the future of IoT.

[ARi Embedded Engineering](https://www.arigs.com/services/embedded-engineering-services/) specializes in overcoming these challenges and delivering cutting-edge embedded solutions. Partner with us to unlock the power of IoT.

# 10 Real Life Examples of Embedded Systems

![Bob Blumenscheid](/getattachment/a79f5cc7-f436-41b0-b5dc-1dd672e796f4/BobBlumenscheid.jpg?lang=en-US&width=400&height=400&ext=.jpg
)

[Get Our White PaperCompare chip-down vs. modular designsDOWNLOAD PDF](https://www.digi.com/resources/library/white-papers/design_vs_build_web)

Embedded systems are at the heart of many different products, machines and intelligent operations, such as machine learning and artificial intelligence applications. As embedded systems applications appear in every industry and sector today, embedded devices and software play a crucial role in the functioning of cars, home appliances, medical devices, interactive kiosks, and other equipment we use in our daily lives. In this article, we have provided embedded system examples with explanations to help you learn how this technology is impacting every facet of modern life.  
   
While real life [embedded systems](https://www.digi.com/products/embedded-systems) have become a significant part of our lives, they are engineered to operate with minimal human intervention. Characteristics like compact size, simple design, and low cost make them a useful technology in industries like aerospace, automotive, healthcare, and even smart cities. Thus, they are one of the driving forces behind today’s digital, connected, and automated world. Here you will find the types and characteristics of embedded systems along with some real-life examples of devices running embedded software.

## 4 Types of Embedded Devices

![Embedded system](/getattachment/Blog/post/Examples-of-Embedded-Systems/GettyImages-1296908133-1280x720.jpg?lang=en-US)

Common embedded systems can be broken into four types based on performance as well as functional requirements:

Real-time embedded systems are designed and installed to carry out specific tasks within a pre-defined time limit. They are further divided into two different types:

### Stand-alone

These are self-sufficient systems that do not rely on a host system like a processor or a computer to perform tasks. Here are some standalone embedded technology examples:

### Networked

These systems are connected to a wired or wireless network to perform assigned tasks and provide output to the connected devices. They are comprised of components like controllers and sensors. Here are some network embedded software examples:

### Mobile

These systems are smaller in size and easy to use. Though they come with limited memory, people still prefer them due to their portability and handiness. Here are a few mobile embedded control systems examples:

## 10 Embedded Systems Examples

There are many things with embedded systems incorporated in the Internet of Things (IoT), as well as in machine to machine (M2M) devices. Exceptionally versatile and adaptable, embedded systems can be found in all smart devices today. It is difficult to find a single portion of modern life that doesn’t involve this technology. Here are some of the real-life examples of embedded system applications.

### 1. Central Heating Systems

![Connected home heating system](/getattachment/Blog/post/Examples-of-Embedded-Systems/GettyImages-520775952-1280x720.jpg?lang=en-US)  
Central heating systems convert the chemical energy into thermal energy in a furnace room and transfer that energy into heat, which is then delivered to numerous spaces within a building. It is important for these systems to have thermostat controls to adjust the temperature, which is achieved by an embedded system.   
 

![Connected home heating system](/getattachment/Blog/post/Examples-of-Embedded-Systems/GettyImages-520775952-1280x720.jpg?lang=en-US)  
Central heating systems convert the chemical energy into thermal energy in a furnace room and transfer that energy into heat, which is then delivered to numerous spaces within a building. It is important for these systems to have thermostat controls to adjust the temperature, which is achieved by an embedded system.   
   

### 2. GPS Systems

![Vehicle GPS system](/getattachment/Blog/post/Examples-of-Embedded-Systems/GettyImages-1259149536-1280x720.jpg?lang=en-US)  
The GPS is a navigation system that uses satellites and receivers to synchronize data related to location, time, and velocity. The receiver or device that receives the data has an integrated embedded system to facilitate the application of a global positioning system. The embedded GPS devices allow people to find their current locations and destinations easily. Thus, they are gaining rapid momentum and becoming the most widely used navigation tools for automobiles.  
   
Nowadays, GPS systems are generally used in:

### 3. Fitness Trackers

![Fitness tracker](/getattachment/Blog/post/Examples-of-Embedded-Systems/GettyImages-1284036952-1280x720.jpg?lang=en-US)  
Fitness trackers are wearable devices that can monitor your health and track activities like sleeping, running, and walking. These devices use embedded systems to garner data related to your heart rate, body temperature, and the number of footsteps, which is further sent to servers via WAN like LTE or GPRS.   
   
Fitness trackers are generally used for:

These devices use embedded systems to garner data related to your heart rate, body temperature, and the number of footsteps, which is further sent to servers via WAN like LTE or GPRS.

### 4. Medical Devices

![Connected medical device](/getattachment/Blog/post/Examples-of-Embedded-Systems/GettyImages-1203126671-1280x720.jpg?lang=en-US)  
Medical devices in healthcare facilities have been incorporating embedded systems for quite some time. A new class of medical devices use embedded systems to help treat patients who need frequent monitoring and constant attention at home. These systems are embedded with sensors to gather data related to patients’ health like heart rate, pulse rate, or readings from implants, which are sent to a cloud where a doctor can review patient data on their device wirelessly. Medical devices have been widely used for diagnosing and treating patients efficiently, and some of their examples are:

### 5. Automotive Systems

![On-board connected vehicle technology](/getattachment/Blog/post/Examples-of-Embedded-Systems/GettyImages-966859036-1280x720.jpg?lang=en-US)  
Automotive embedded systems are designed and installed to enhance the safety of automobiles. Thanks to the safety systems in vehicles, the traffic fatality rate has plummeted in recent years. Automobile industries are going the extra mile to reinforce automobiles with advanced technology systems and sensors, which is not possible without embedded systems.  
   
Some key examples of an active safety system include adaptive speed control, car breakdown warning, pedestrian recognition, merging assistance, airbags, and more. These are a few features anticipated to mitigate the risk of accidents and foster the demand for embedded systems across the globe.  
   
Some more examples of automotive embedded systems include:

### 6. Transit and Fare Collection

![Automated transit fare collection](/getattachment/Blog/post/Examples-of-Embedded-Systems/GettyImages-1187010833-1280x720.jpg?lang=en-US)  
Automated Fare Collection (AFC) is a ticketing system that allows passengers to pay the fare through ticket vending machines or online services. These systems were originated with coins and tokens but have been replaced with magnetic stripe cards or smart cards. An AFC is a basic station device comprising a ticket vending machine, automatic gate machine, and ticket checking machine. These components are embedded systems that ensure faster transactions, seamless operations, and more efficient payment collection.  
   
While city transit bus and commuter rails still use paper tickets and passes, urban transit systems have adopted AFC with smart cards, which are inexpensive technologies and offer additional security along with data collection options.  
   
Automated fare collection systems are generally found at:

### 7. ATMs

![Connected ATM machine](/getattachment/Blog/post/Examples-of-Embedded-Systems/GettyImages-1058710486-1280x720.jpg?lang=en-US)  
An automated teller machine (ATM) is a computerized machine used in banking that communicates with a host bank computer over a network. The bank computer verifies all the data entered by the users and stores all transactions, while the embedded system in the ATM displays the transaction data and processes inputs from the ATM keyboard.  
   
An ATM is mostly used to:

![Connected ATM machine](/getattachment/Blog/post/Examples-of-Embedded-Systems/GettyImages-1058710486-1280x720.jpg?lang=en-US)

### 8. Factory Robots

![Factory robots](/getattachment/Blog/post/Examples-of-Embedded-Systems/GettyImages-1219401451-1280x720.jpg?lang=en-US)  
Factory robots are designed to perform high-precision tasks under dangerous work conditions. They have an integrated embedded system to connect different subsystems. In a typical mechanical job, robots employ actuators, sensors, and software to perceive the environment and derive intended output safely.   
   
Without an embedded system, robots would have to rely on external control or computing systems. This, in turn, can elevate the safety risks due to delay or failure in the connection link between the factory robot and its external computing system. Today, as [Industry 4.0](https://www.digi.com/blog/post/12-fun-facts-about-iot-2021) comes to fruition, these systems are integrating artificial intelligence and machine learning to make equipment smarter, safer and more effective — for example, enabling machines to identify defects that the human eye wouldn't see, and remove these from production.  
   
Factory robots have a range of applications:

![Factory robots](/getattachment/Blog/post/Examples-of-Embedded-Systems/GettyImages-1219401451-1280x720.jpg?lang=en-US)

### 9. Electric Vehicle Charging Stations

![Electric vehicle charging stations](/getattachment/Blog/post/Examples-of-Embedded-Systems/GettyImages-1221447606-1280x720.jpg?lang=en-US)  
Electric vehicle charging stations are equipped with charging points or units that supply electric power to charge connected vehicles. An embedded system resides in the charging station to provide processing for graphics displays, report any issues with the device and alert technicians when maintenance is required. This embedded solution provides an easy and cost-effective approach to monitoring and maintaining the charging infrastructure. A number of Digi customers, such as [AddÉnergie](https://www.digi.com/customer-stories/addenergie-driving-electric-vehicle-charging-stati), are developing solutions to serve this growing market.  
  
Some of the common uses of electric vehicle charging stations include:

![Electric vehicle charging stations](/getattachment/Blog/post/Examples-of-Embedded-Systems/GettyImages-1221447606-1280x720.jpg?lang=en-US)

### 10. Interactive Kiosks

![Interactive kiosk](/getattachment/Blog/post/Examples-of-Embedded-Systems/GettyImages-821840638-1280x720.jpg?lang=en-US)  
Self-service kiosks are designed to offer services and information to end-users in environments where human employee presence isn’t possible or cost-effective. For instance, these machines and terminals allow a passenger in an empty airport to buy a meal at 4 am without interacting with human workers. Interactive kiosks come in all shapes and sizes, from simple coffee dispensing systems to complex vending machines and fuel stations with high-definition graphics. For this reason, it is important for embedded developers to work with a scalable product line like [Digi ConnectCore® 8X/8M system-on-modules (SOMs)](/products/embedded-systems/digi-connectcore/system-on-modules), which support development of product lines with scaling levels of functionality.  
   
An embedded system provides the processing for connected, self-service kiosk machines, offering an interactive consumer experience. These systems can be developed to function in remote and outdoor environments and deliver information and services even in extreme weather conditions. They can also eliminate downtime for real-time applications and have expandable I/O options designed for workload consolidation.  
   
Apart from airports, interactive kiosk machines are mostly found in:

![Interactive kiosk](/getattachment/Blog/post/Examples-of-Embedded-Systems/GettyImages-821840638-1280x720.jpg?lang=en-US)

## The Importance of Embedded Systems

![Vending machine](/getattachment/Blog/post/Examples-of-Embedded-Systems/GettyImages-1047325348-1280x720.jpg?lang=en-US)  
People often question the importance of embedded systems in daily life. These small computers are integrated into various systems for performing specific image and data processing tasks, and thus significantly impact the way we commute, spend our leisure time, run business, and perform various other daily activities.  
  
Still wondering where can you find embedded systems? Whether you work in a restaurant, office, health practice, factory or other environment, your workplace is full of systems that are built with embedded devices, like elevators, printers, routers and point of sale systems. In brief, they can be found everywhere in our society — from vehicles and EV charging stations to vending machines, lottery ticket systems and digital signage to sophisticated medical equipment.  
  
Here are some of the reasons why embedded systems are becoming prevalent across the globe:

![Vending machine](/getattachment/Blog/post/Examples-of-Embedded-Systems/GettyImages-1047325348-1280x720.jpg?lang=en-US)

Additionally, embedded modules are becoming more sophisticated and powerful all the time, and are increasing in graphics performance and edge compute capabilities, giving embedded developers the tools to bring high-performance market-driven products to market.

## Find Your Embedded Systems Solutions with Digi

 The significance of embedded systems is so much that the world without them would look considerably different than it does today. Thanks to the continuous tech advancements, they will become more crucial for every device in the foreseeable future. Understanding why we use embedded systems and a plethora of examples where they are installed will make you better equipped to perceive the tech world around you and leverage the benefits of this exciting technology.

# Top Uses and Applications of Embedded Systems in Automation

![](https://secure.gravatar.com/avatar/6191728ce2c51340cb909368d74dbc51dff20884fa2653f783571f4e957cf9e9?s=32&d=mm&r=g)

*Written By*

The IoT Academy

*Written By*

The IoT Academy

*Published on* August 3rd, 2023

*Updated on* June 26, 2025

The automation of industrial processes is being altered by the rise of embedded systems applications as a crucial component of many sectors. Manufacturing procedures are simplified, performance is improved, and efficiency is optimized by them. The integration of these systems into equipment and machinery is fruitful. Such systems consist of hardware and software components to complete particular jobs within a larger system.

They are useful in many modern products, including medical equipment, mobile phones, and aircraft. These systems need them to carry out a single specified function. Read on to learn about embedded systems applications in automation and how they improve industries.

## What are Embedded Systems?

A microcontroller-based system created to carry out a specific task is an embedded system. It is a hybrid of software and hardware for computers. It can operate on its own or as a component of a large system. A single-chip microcontroller, such as a Cortex, ARM, DSP, or microprocessor, is present in these systems. Its attributes include power, size, dependability, accuracy, and adaptability. Also, it differs from a workstation server in its design, price, function, and human participation. One should test any embedded system to make sure it is of the highest quality. It should match all requirements for automation.

Also, automation testing software ensures the security of these instruments in crucial applications. Examples include those in the automotive, medical, and aviation industries. Automation [embedded systems](https://www.theiotacademy.co/blog/embedded-system-overview) are one of the well-known applications of embedded systems. End-to-end testing is also required to make sure the program functions as planned from start to finish.

### Why are Embedded Systems Important to Industrial Automation?

Embedded systems in industrial automation offer a wide range of advantages and capabilities. Industries can improve operations, make educated decisions, and act fast in the event of abnormalities. All this is possible due to real-time control and monitoring capabilities. They become more productive and efficient by automating repetitive tasks and optimizing processes. The embedded system provides proactive monitoring with an emphasis on safety.

It allows the incorporation of cutting-edge technologies like AI, ML, and IoT. The system offers fast hazard detection and effective risk management. Also, it opens up new possibilities for complex analytics. It enhances preventative maintenance and autonomous decision-making. The embedded system is a crucial part of industrial automation, encouraging innovation. It allows firms to flourish in a dynamic and harsh environment. You may go for an **[embedded systems course](https://www.theiotacademy.co/embedded-systems-training)** to learn more about its automation application.

### Use of Embedded System in Industrial Automation

Machine control and machine monitoring are the two primary areas of industrial automation embedded systems.

**1. Machine Control**

It is one of the primary applications of embedded systems in industrial automation. It allows control over various procedures. One can control manufacturing devices, sensors, and equipment. They can be coordinated using embedded technology acting as the primary control point. These systems take data from sensors as input, process it, and then output signals. It helps to control the operation of actuators, motors, valves, and other parts. Embedded systems carry out industrial processes by controlling and optimizing.

**2. Machine Monitoring**

Monitoring in industrial automation also makes use of embedded systems. They have interfaces and sensors that make it possible to gather real-time data from many locations. They can gather data inside the production environment. Information on temperature, pressure, humidity, and vibration can be included in this data. With the help of ML and deep learning algorithms, embedded systems process and analyze this data. It provides important information about the operation, condition, and health of machinery and processes.

Embedded systems enable proactive decision-making and early failure detection. They support predictive maintenance through the continuous monitoring of key variables. This increases operational efficiency, and dependability and reduces downtime. With the help of an embedded testing course, one can learn how to test and improve the efficiency of machines.

### Automation Applications Using Embedded Systems

For embedded applications in automated systems, embedded systems are perfect. Automated systems aim to speed up production and other activities. They help lower the possibility of human error and enhance operator safety. Automation apps use flash memory to run processes. Devices that host automation applications can be compact without compromising dependability. Embedded systems must endure the environmental demands of manufacturing facilities. Extreme temperature ranges, vibration, and shock are frequent in these conditions. Single-level cell (SLC) flash memory is there in many embedded systems for automation applications.

Applications for embedded systems range from low-cost to high-priced consumer electronics. It includes industrial equipment, medical devices, missile control systems, and aerospace systems. You may find it in entertainment equipment and academic equipment as well. Embedded systems affect all aspects of modern life. The following are examples of embedded systems in automation:

You can appreciate them for their part in your life even before you open your eyes in the morning. It is crucial to protect you, your family, and your belongings. Hence home security and alarm systems use embedded technology. A complex home alarm system comprises sensors that can identify problems. A microcontroller is there that can interpret the data. Also, there is an output system, which is an auditory alarm.

equipment for fuel injection controllers, GPS, anti-lock brakes, and airbag systems. Engine management and control is one of the most important applications for embedded systems in cars. These technologies track the engine's operation for various things. For instance, high fuel efficiency, lower emissions, and improved performance.

Copy machines, fax machines, modems, printers, and scanners are some examples. They may differ in complexity like an elevator only needs a few algorithms. Whereas machine control and monitoring systems in industrial plants might need complex fuzzy logic. They need accurate sensors and expert algorithms. But they all serve to highlight the significance of embedded systems in contemporary business.

Includes things like navigational systems, space explorers, and robotic spacecraft. It also involves automatic landing systems and flight attitude controls. Embedded systems play crucial roles within the aerospace and military industries. These systems adhere to strict reliability, security, and performance standards.

ECG, EMG, MRI, EEG, CT scanner, BP check, and glucose tracking are examples of medical diagnostic equipment. Healthcare is one industry where embedded systems are useful. They are helpful in almost all kinds of imaging systems, including PET scans, CT scans, and MRIs. They are useful to check vital signs and amplify sounds in electronic stethoscopes. For better results, they also enhance images. These systems are also used by glucose meters, pacemakers, CPAP devices, and several biomedical sensors. Through telemedicine and other remote systems, they enable doctors to track patients' health. Hence, they decide on further diagnoses and treatments.

These tools include an ATM, a smart vending machine, and a stock exchange. They reduce the regulatory complexity and hazards related to the new financial services products. Embedded banking offers payment companies and banks advantages. For instance, improved client stickiness, access to new jurisdictions, lower costs, and quicker time to market.

IP, hub, router, cell phone, web camera Phone Mobile phones, data routers, and network switches all use embedded technology. High-speed networking capabilities are also improved by the technology. Embedded programming has more applications than you can think.

To synchronize information about location, time, and velocity, the GPS navigation system makes use of satellites and receivers. For the use of a GPS, the receiver or device that receives the data has an embedded system built in. People may locate their present locations via the incorporated GPS gadgets. Hence, they are taking the top spot among automotive navigation systems. An embedded training institute may not teach a particular application but will clear your understanding.

Through ticket vending machines or online services, travelers can pay their fares using the automated fare collection (AFC) system. These systems can now use magnetic stripe cards or smart cards using coins and tokens. The basic station equipment known as an AFC consists of a ticket vending machine. It is an automatic gate machine and a ticket-checking machine. These elements guarantee quicker transactions. You get seamless operations and more effective payment collection.

It includes pressure monitoring systems, assembly lines, voltage, and temperature. The current detecting systems, as well as data collection systems, are also there. As the need for automation grows, embedded systems play a crucial role. These systems can boost industrial processes' productivity, efficiency, and innovation.

These stations have charging units or ports that deliver electricity to power-linked automobiles. They process graphics displays and report any problems with the device. As a result, it notifies staff when maintenance is necessary. There is an embedded system in the charging station for it. The monitoring and upkeep of the charging infrastructure is simple through it. You can learn embedded systems and make a fantastic career in the above fields.

#### Conclusion

As the need for automation grows, embedded systems are there to play a significant role in industrial automation. These systems can boost industrial processes' productivity, efficiency, and innovation. Moreover, applications of embedded systems would be further increased through integration with cutting-edge technologies. They include the Internet of Things (IoT) and artificial intelligence (AI). These systems are helping firms succeed in the dynamic world of industrial automation. To know more, join the **Embedded Systems Course** from **The IoT Academy!**

enriched_points.expanded_content: Embedded systems enable smart home devices like thermostats, lighting, and security systems to interact seamlessly, enhancing convenience, energy efficiency, and security through automation and remote control.
In healthcare, embedded systems power devices such as wearables and monitoring equipment, facilitating real-time health tracking, remote diagnostics, and timely alerts for medical professionals.
Embedded systems in industrial automation streamline operations by controlling machinery, monitoring processes, and enabling predictive maintenance, thus optimizing productivity and enhancing safety.
Agricultural embedded systems monitor soil moisture, temperature, and crop health, allowing for data-driven irrigation and fertilization, ultimately boosting yield and resource efficiency.

Wearable devices, powered by embedded systems, track fitness metrics and health parameters, providing users with insights and promoting healthier lifestyles through continuous monitoring and data analysis.
